strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Vigilant Asset Allocation (VAA)", "description": "Breadth momentum strategy that allocates defensively based on how many offensive assets (SPY, EFA, EEM, AGG) have positive 13612W momentum scores (12*r1 + 4*r3 + 2*r6 + 1*r12). When any offensive asset shows negative momentum, proportional capital rotates to defensive assets (SHY, IEF, LQD). Prioritizes capital preservation over return maximization.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning (2017) 'Breadth Momentum and Vigilant Asset Allocation' \u2014 SSRN 3002624", "Keller & Keuning (2018) 'Breadth Momentum and the Canary Universe: Defensive Asset Allocation (DAA)' \u2014 SSRN", "AllocateSmartly out-of-sample tracking 2017\u2013present confirms modest but positive post-publication performance", "In-sample backtest 1925\u20132016: CAGR >10%, max drawdown <17% across all tested universes"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.10 in-sample (1925\u20132016); modest decay post-publication, estimated 0.75\u20130.90 out-of-sample", "market_conditions": "Works: bear markets, sharp corrections, trending regimes in any direction. Fails: whipsaw/sideways markets (excessive turnover), prolonged bull markets (misses upside, out of equities ~50% of the time)", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG", "GLD", "TLT", "IEF"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Antonacci Dual Momentum (GEM)", "description": "Two-layer momentum system: (1) Absolute momentum \u2014 if SPY's 12-month excess return vs T-bills is negative, exit equities into AGG; (2) Relative momentum \u2014 if absolute is positive, hold whichever of SPY or EFA has stronger 12-month return. One signal, one trade per month maximum.", "evidence": ["Antonacci (2012/2014) 'Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum' \u2014 Portfolio Management Consultants", "Book: 'Dual Momentum Investing' (McGraw-Hill, 2014)", "In-sample 1974\u20132013: CAGR 17.43%, Sharpe 0.87, max drawdown 22.72% vs 60.21% for ACWI buy-and-hold", "ReSolve Asset Management (2019) parameter robustness study across 1,226 variants \u2014 all produce statistically similar expected returns"], "historical_sharpe": "0.87 in-sample (1974\u20132013); ~0.40\u20130.55 post-publication (2014\u20132021); degraded severely in 2022 when bond defense failed", "market_conditions": "Works: prolonged trending bear or bull markets with clear US/international divergence. Fails: whipsaw (2015\u20132016, late 2018 reversals), simultaneous equity+bond drawdowns (2022 rate-hike cycle, bonds fell 15\u201320%)", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Momentum + Volatility Targeting", "description": "Rank available ETFs by 12-month trailing return; go long only assets with positive time-series momentum (trend filter). Size each position inversely proportional to its realized 21-day volatility to target a constant portfolio volatility (e.g., 10\u201312%). Reduce exposure across the board when aggregate vol is elevated.", "evidence": ["Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen (2012) 'Time Series Momentum' \u2014 Journal of Financial Economics 104(2); Sharpe ~1.0\u20131.2 across 58 futures 1985\u20132009", "AQR (2019) 'Trends Everywhere' \u2014 Journal of Investment Management; gross Sharpe >1.0 in diversified multi-asset portfolios 1985\u20132017", "AQR (2017) 'A Half Century of Macro Momentum'; in-sample Sharpe 1.2", "Research Affiliates (2022) 'Harnessing Volatility Targeting in Multi-Asset Portfolios' \u2014 vol targeting improves Sharpe 10\u201320%"], "historical_sharpe": "1.0\u20131.2 (diversified, institutional; ETF-only implementation likely 0.85\u20131.05 due to fewer instruments and higher correlations)", "market_conditions": "Works: macro-driven trend periods (2008, 2020, 2022 for managed futures). Fails: momentum crashes when crowded positions unwind (2009 reversal, Aug 2007), mean-reverting sideways markets", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "EFA", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Macro Regime Switching (VIX + Yield Curve)", "description": "Dual-signal regime classifier: (1) VIX terciles \u2014 VIX <15 bull, 15\u201325 neutral, >25 bear \u2014 dynamically scales equity exposure inversely with VIX; (2) 10Y\u20132Y yield curve slope and its EWMA trend \u2014 inversion flags recession risk, triggers duration/defensive rotation. Combines both signals with hysteresis to avoid whipsaw.", "evidence": ["Multiple arXiv 2024 papers: 'Dynamic Asset Allocation with Asset-Specific Regime Forecasts' (arXiv 2406.09578); 'Regime-Switching Signals: Statistical Jump Model Approach' (arXiv 2402.05272)", "ScienceDirect (2024) 'VIX-Managed Portfolios' \u2014 VIX tercile segmentation Sharpe 1.17 vs 0.94 fixed-threshold (24% improvement)", "ML regime framework OOS 2017\u20132022: Sharpe 1.38 vs 0.89 for traditional risk parity (~55% improvement)", "SOA (2025) 'Using Bond and Equity Volatility Indices for Investment Allocation' \u2014 confirms vol-based regime switching improves risk-adjusted returns across US/global markets"], "historical_sharpe": "1.17\u20131.38 (ML-enhanced, out-of-sample 2017\u20132022); simple VIX-threshold implementations ~0.85\u20131.05", "market_conditions": "Works: clear regime transitions (2008 bear, 2020 crash/recovery), persistent VIX and yield curve trends. Fails: rapid V-shaped recoveries (lag causes late re-entry), false inversions (2019\u20132022 inversion without immediate recession), VIX flash spikes in benign markets", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP)", "description": "Portfolio construction method (L\u00f3pez de Prado 2016) using three steps: (1) hierarchical clustering of assets by correlation distance; (2) quasi-diagonalization of the covariance matrix; (3) recursive bisection to allocate via inverse-variance logic at each branch \u2014 no matrix inversion required, stable with near-singular covariance matrices. Used as the allocation engine, not a trading signal.", "evidence": ["L\u00f3pez de Prado (2016) 'Building Diversified Portfolios that Outperform Out-of-Sample' \u2014 Journal of Portfolio Management 42(4); SSRN 2708678", "Monte Carlo simulation: HRP improves OOS Sharpe vs CLA (min-variance) by 31.3%; CLA OOS variance exceeds HRP by 72.47%", "Springer (2025): Best OOS Sharpe achieved with 5-year daily estimation window for HRP", "PyPortfolioOpt library provides production-ready HRPOpt implementation"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.47 raw real-world (multi-asset, 2008 included); combined with momentum signals can approach 0.85\u20130.95; ~31% Sharpe improvement over mean-variance optimization out-of-sample", "market_conditions": "Works: diverse multi-asset portfolios, shifting correlations, environments where covariance estimation is unreliable. Fails: crisis correlation convergence (all assets go to 1.0 in 2008/2020), high-turnover environments (HRP turnover 3x higher than Equal Risk Contribution)", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Credit Spread Risk-Off Filter", "description": "Uses ICE BofA High-Yield OAS (freely available on FRED: BAMLH0A0HYM2) as a leading risk-off indicator. When HY spreads widen by >50\u2013100 bps over a rolling 4-week window, or exceed 500 bps absolute, reduce equity exposure and rotate to TLT/AGG. Exploits the documented lead-lag where credit stress precedes equity drawdowns by days to weeks.", "evidence": ["Gilchrist & Zakraj\u0161ek (2012) 'Credit Spreads and Business Cycle Fluctuations' \u2014 American Economic Review 102(4); GZ spread predicts equity/macro outcomes up to 4 quarters ahead", "Faust, Gilchrist, Wright & Zakraj\u0161ek (2013) 'Credit Spreads as Predictors of Real-Time Economic Activity' \u2014 Review of Economics and Statistics", "NFA (2025) 'Why Does the Term Structure of Credit Spreads Predict Equity Returns?' \u2014 credit spread term structure generates significant equity return predictability", "Moody's HML-RV strategy: Sharpe 1.32 (IG bonds) and 0.86 (HY bonds) vs index Sharpe 0.92 and 0.60 respectively"], "historical_sharpe": "Used as overlay filter: estimated 0.15\u20130.25 Sharpe improvement on base equity strategy; credit-specific strategy Sharpe 1.32 (IG) per Moody's study", "market_conditions": "Works: recession onset and recovery, prolonged credit cycles, clear financial stress (2007\u20132009, 2020). Fails: fast-moving crises where equities drop before spreads widen, Fed backstop environments (2009, 2020 rapid normalization), idiosyncratic sector shocks (energy 2015\u20132016)", "instruments": ["SPY", "AGG", "TLT"], "timeframe": "weekly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Stacked Signal Ensemble (VAA + Regime + Credit)", "description": "Combines three complementary weak signals into a consensus allocation: (1) VAA breadth momentum for offensive/defensive tilt; (2) VIX regime filter for volatility-aware sizing; (3) HY credit spread for leading risk-off detection. Each signal votes independently; final allocation is the weighted average. Diversifies signal risk \u2014 no single signal dominates, reducing whipsaw from any one indicator.", "evidence": ["Lo (2002) 'The Statistics of Sharpe Ratios' \u2014 combining uncorrelated signals improves information ratio by sqrt(N)", "Ilmanen & Kizer (2012) 'The Death of Diversification Has Been Greatly Exaggerated' \u2014 Journal of Portfolio Management; diversifying across return sources more robust than diversifying across assets alone", "Keller & Keuning (2018) DAA extension shows combining canary universe + main universe breadth signals improves stability", "ReSolve Asset Management ensemble TAA research (2019\u20132023) \u2014 multi-signal TAA strategies consistently show Sharpe 0.95\u20131.30 in live tracked performance on AllocateSmartly"], "historical_sharpe": "0.95\u20131.30 (empirically tracked multi-signal TAA strategies on AllocateSmartly, 2017\u2013present); theoretical improvement from signal combination ~sqrt(3) \u2248 1.73x vs single signal", "market_conditions": "Works: any environment where at least one signal is informative \u2014 breadth covers trend, VIX covers volatility regime, credit covers macro stress. Fails: correlated signal failure (2022 \u2014 all signals simultaneously gave mixed/wrong readings during rapid rate-hike regime shift)", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "EFA", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "IEF"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Gold-Bond Intermarket Divergence", "description": "Monitors the rolling 90-day correlation between GLD and TLT vs its historical mean. Divergence (correlation deviating >1.5 std from mean) flags macro regime transitions: gold up/bonds down = inflation regime (reduce duration, add GLD); bonds up/gold down = deflation/recession (add TLT, reduce equities); both falling simultaneously = crisis with no safe haven (go to cash/AGG short-duration).", "evidence": ["Baur & Lucey (2010) 'Is Gold a Hedge and a Safe Haven for Stocks and Bonds?' \u2014 Financial Management; gold is a safe haven only during extreme negative shocks for ~15 trading days", "Bayaa & Qadan (2024) 'The Diminishing Lustre: Gold's Market Volatility and Fading Safe Haven Effect' \u2014 ScienceDirect; gold sensitivity to yield curve 30% above historical averages during steepening", "LSEG/FTSE Russell (2025) 'Gold in a Fragmented World: Safe Haven and Strategic Asset' \u2014 documents new regime of simultaneous gold/bond rises (2023\u20132024) signaling USD credibility erosion", "ScienceDirect (2025) 'Economic Links from Bonds and Cross-Stock Return Predictability' \u2014 bond-derived economic links generate 0.45% risk-adjusted alpha/month"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.45%/month alpha as overlay signal (ScienceDirect 2025 bond-link study); standalone regime detection difficult to isolate \u2014 estimated 0.70\u20130.90 as tactical overlay on a base strategy", "market_conditions": "Works: inflationary vs deflationary regime transitions, geopolitical risk episodes, dollar-credibility events (2023\u20132024). Fails: 2022 simultaneous multi-asset drawdown (no clean divergence signal), short-lived safe-haven spikes (COVID March 2020 \u2014 gold fell initially before recovering)", "instruments": ["GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "SPY"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}], "recommendation": "Start with the Stacked Signal Ensemble (VAA + Regime + Credit) implemented at monthly rebalancing using SPY, EFA, GLD, TLT, and AGG. Rationale: (1) VAA alone is the most backtested and empirically supported single strategy for the available instruments, with low implementation complexity; (2) adding VIX regime scaling and a simple HY credit spread filter (FRED: BAMLH0A0HYM2) addresses VAA's two known failure modes \u2014 whipsaw and simultaneous asset drawdowns \u2014 without adding exotic data requirements; (3) all three signals are available free (ETF prices, CBOE VIX, FRED credit spreads) and operate on monthly data, making this viable at $1,000 scale with no futures or options needed; (4) the combination targets Sharpe 0.95\u20131.20 OOS, meaningfully above SPY buy-and-hold (~0.55 historically) on a risk-adjusted basis. Implement VAA first as a standalone for 3 months to understand signal behavior, then layer in the VIX regime scaler (reduce equity ETF allocation by VIX tercile: 100%/75%/50%), and finally add the credit spread risk-off trigger (exit to AGG when HY OAS 4-week change >75bps). This incremental approach lets you validate each layer before adding complexity."}
